title: [ENHANCEMENT] Add z-loss
body:
z-loss regularization was shown to stabilize training by preventing logits in the last layer to explode: https://arxiv.org/abs/2309.14322

This PR: 
- allows one to add zloss regularization
- since the total loss is not cross entropy anymore but cross entropy + zloss, this PR also allows one to additionally log the original cross-entropy and the z-loss

step_plan.summary: Add optional z-loss regularization to language-model training by augmenting the optimization loss as CE + (z_loss_coeff × z-loss) while separately reporting the original cross-entropy and the z-loss term. Wire the new coefficient through CLI args, compute z-loss where logits are available (core and legacy), and ensure training aggregation/logging surfaces the three metrics consistently.

steps.operation: Register CLI flag `--z-loss-coeff` and expose it on `args`
steps.reason: Training needs a user-configurable coefficient to enable/disable z-loss regularization (default 0.0). Placing it in the training arg group keeps it alongside other optimization/loss knobs and distinct from existing MoE router z-loss flags.
steps.side_effects: Adds a new public CLI surface area; requires updating any docs/examples that enumerate supported args. No behavioral change when left at default.

steps.operation: Thread new argument through the argument parsing path so downstream modules can read `args.z_loss_coeff`
steps.reason: Ensures the new flag is actually parsed and present in the returned args/config object used by model/loss code paths.
steps.side_effects: If this repo snapshots/prints args for reproducibility, output will include the new field; otherwise no runtime impact.

steps.operation: Validate and normalize z-loss settings (non-negative, default behavior)
steps.reason: Prevents silent misconfiguration (e.g., negative coeff) and guarantees baseline equivalence when `z_loss_coeff==0`.
steps.side_effects: Invalid configs that previously would have run will now error early; may need to adjust existing launcher scripts if they pass unexpected values.

steps.operation: Compute per-token z-loss from logits alongside CE and return an optimization loss that includes it
steps.reason: This is the centralized Megatron-Core loss primitive that already computes vocab-parallel cross entropy; adding z-loss here minimizes duplication and keeps behavior consistent for fused/unfused CE paths while having access to logits. It should produce (a) CE tokens, (b) z-loss tokens, and (c) total tokens = CE + coeff*z.
steps.side_effects: Must be tensor-parallel safe: if z-loss depends on a full-vocab `logsumexp`, it may require an all-reduce or a vocab-parallel logsumexp utility. Any change to the return structure may require adjusting callers; prefer preserving the primary return as the optimization loss tensor and exposing CE/z via an auxiliary dict/side-channel to avoid widespread breakage.

steps.operation: Plumb CE and z-loss reporting from the model forward so the training step can log both while optimizing total loss
steps.reason: `GPTModel.forward` is the point where logits exist (post-process stage) and where loss outputs are formed when labels are provided. It should ensure the optimization target is total loss, while also returning (or otherwise making available) separate scalars/tensors for original CE and z-loss for logging.
steps.side_effects: Potentially changes the forward output contract (e.g., returning an additional loss dict). Any custom `forward_step_func` implementations or downstream wrappers may need updates to consume the extra metrics; must not break pipeline-parallel stages that don’t have logits (only last stage should compute/report these losses).

steps.operation: Aggregate microbatch metrics for total/CE/z-loss consistently across data-parallel and pipeline-parallel execution
steps.reason: Once forward steps emit separate keys (e.g., `lm loss`, `z loss`, `loss`/`total loss`), `train_step` must reduce/accumulate them correctly (including existing tuple (numerator,denominator) conventions) so logging reports meaningful averages.
steps.side_effects: Incorrect handling can skew reported values or break logging when mixing scalar and (num,den) formats. May require updating any tests or scripts that expect a fixed set of loss_dict keys.

steps.operation: Ensure training logs include optimization loss plus separate CE and z-loss terms with stable naming
steps.reason: `training_log` is the centralized sink that prints/writes whatever is in `loss_dict`. Confirm it logs the new keys (and, if necessary, add formatting/aliasing so users see `total loss`, `lm loss` (CE), and `z loss` clearly).
steps.side_effects: Log output and TensorBoard/W&B series will gain new curves; dashboards/alerts that assume only `lm loss` may need adjustments. Minimal runtime overhead.

steps.operation: Add z-loss to the legacy GPT loss path and expose separate CE and z-loss metrics for logging
steps.reason: Legacy training entrypoints compute logits and CE here; parity requires applying the same regularization and reporting so users get consistent behavior regardless of core vs legacy model path.
steps.side_effects: Legacy loss semantics will change when `z_loss_coeff>0` (optimization target differs). Must ensure tensor-parallel correctness in legacy vocab-parallel setup and that any legacy-specific loss dict keys align with the new logging conventions.

steps.operation: Extend inference/training loss wrapper to optimize total loss while reporting CE and z-loss (patterned after existing `lm loss` reporting)
steps.reason: This utility already follows the pattern of returning an optimization loss distinct from the reported `lm loss` (e.g., for KD). Adding z-loss here ensures any workflows that rely on `loss_func` (evaluation, specialized training, export-time loss computation) can enable z-loss and still log CE and z-loss separately.
steps.side_effects: May change reported metrics in inference/eval outputs when enabled; must ensure default (`z_loss_coeff==0`) yields identical results. If this path is used in scripts expecting a fixed metric set, they may need to tolerate the extra keys.
